rows.source_ref: Proc, "Previous Day Breakout Edge System", ForexFactory thread 1075281, ~2021, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1075281/previous-day-breakout-edge-system
rows.concept: A confirmed break-and-close of the previous day's high (long) or low (short) on the H1 chart signals continuation, entered at the next candle's open with a fixed 1:2 R:R and an optional 34-SMA trend filter.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic prior-day-level breakout, pure price rules in MQL5, fixed SL/TP with defined risk, EURUSD/GBPUSD data available, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
rows.notes: Core OP rules: trading day starts 22:00 GMT (cyclic day boundary); enter at open of the H1 candle AFTER an H1 close beyond prev-day high/low; SL 12.5 pips / TP 25 pips (R:R 1:2). Optional 34-SMA filter (price above SMA for longs). Author claims ~57% WR but only with discretionary fundamental/news overlay; Maddin's mechanical M30 backtest EU 2020-2021 (1 trade/day, extended London 06:00-17:00 GMT-1, SL13/TP26) showed -3R over 13 months -> edge unproven; klw notes popular static breakout unlikely to hold. Variants (one core, treat as params to sweep at Q02): ATR-based SL/TP + breakeven (NikoArms ATR(72)); wider stops for gold/USA100 (SL20/TP30); 'false-breakout' re-entry filter (MachineLearn, judged not worth it); '3-Day-Levels' trend filter; yonnie's pre-breakout 25-35% ATR pullback entry. Reported to extend to GBPJPY, USA100/NDX, XAUUSD but best-specified on FX. Reputability: anonymous FF thread, no shared audited stats (Balke-precedent keeps it in scope). DaveBerg's late post proposes a DISTINCT inside-range mean-reversion concept (trade BETWEEN prev-day H/L, sell near prev-day-avg-to-high when weekly/monthly candle bearish) but it is VAGUE (needs unspecified patterns/news/variables) -> not separately implementable.
